target: 9. Another Ending
Hizuru Minakata
August 20
This is the end of my time on Hitogashima Island.

(The days since then felt
both long and short…)

(At the very least, my life
has completely changed.)

(Since then, I haven't heard
the straw voice anymore.
It must have returned to the future.)
You're leaving, huh?
Nezu…

Stop calling me by my name like that!
No matter how much I say it, you don't understand.
Don't worry about it.
I am worrying!

Hmph… well, okay.
Where are you going?

I've decided to move to Tokyo.
My father has something arranged there.

Tokyo, huh?
It's so far away.

My parents said it's better…
I've found this island too painful to stay on.

There's nothing you can do about it.
What about you, though?
Aren't you the same?

I don't have such feelings myself.
Rather…

It's because I didn't let Ryunosuke stay on this island.
That's where it is.
It seems I still can't believe that story.

There's nothing to be done.
It's such an absurd story.

You're right.
It's common to say that the deceased
live on in one's heart…

But saying that a dead person actually lives inside your body
is hard to believe at first.

But on this island,
there are things that shouldn't exist
that I've actually seen.
So that's how it is…

I can't leave that monster unchecked.
I'll keep hunting that monster on this island.
Even against a monster, you have a hunter's mindset.

I already know how to defeat it.
How could I let something like that get the better of me?

Eventually, I'll figure out their habits,
effective attacks,
and even their weapons.
Of course, that's only if it doesn't die too quickly.

That's right…
But I don't think we'll meet them
very often in the future.
Why do you say that?

They probably have been on this island
for a long time.

Yet they've remained unknown to most,
so they've probably been avoiding attention
and refraining from flashy activities.
That's why they don't attack people randomly.

This island is small.
If one person disappears,
it becomes a big deal.
It would be widely known immediately.

That's why the monsters don't act openly.
That's the same for Haine too.

That's why they avoid people.
That's why they're hungry for people.
I see…

The monster you're dealing with, Nezu,
is more troublesome than a starving carnivore.
Be careful.

Don't worry,
you won't be as reckless as me.

When I have time, I'll write a letter.
A letter is better than email or phone calls.

If you feel like it, you can also tell me
about your hunting results via letter.
What would I do with that?

It's someone who went through all that.
It's not strange to want to gather information
about them, is it?

Hmph…
Well, if you feel like it.

Ah, I appreciate it.
That day, Haine…
There's still so much I don't understand.

I should go now.
My parents are waiting.
You won't be coming back, you know.

Ah, if this story
allows me to do that…
You're saying something weird again.
Then, goodbye.

Thank you for saving me that day.
I'm sure I'll continue to be grateful
for this encounter for a long time.

…Hmph, when you say that,
it feels like there's something hidden.

That's rude.
But if you take it that way,
it'll be easier for me too.

Then, this time for real,
it's goodbye.
Ah, take care.

(Asao came to see me off,
waving her hand while crying…)
(Where is Nezu… already gone?)

Hitogashima Island…
It's getting smaller and smaller.

(From here on, I'll have to
carve out my own future)

(And the path I walk from now on
will one day…)
(Connect back to that island, I suppose)

(Yes…
Regardless of whether I want it or not)

Goodbye, Haine.
Next time we meet…

It'd be nice if we could fight
honorably and openly.
